steps.title: Identify the appositive and existing punctuation
steps.content: The phrase “a country in Western Asia” is a non-restrictive appositive that describes Bahrain. The sentence already contains a dash after this phrase (before “is”), signaling we need matching punctuation before it.

steps.title: Apply the em dash rule for appositives
steps.content: Em dashes set off a non-restrictive appositive when used in matching pairs. Placing an em dash before and after “a country in Western Asia” creates “Bahrain—a country in Western Asia—is...” which correctly frames the additional information about Bahrain. The other choices either omit punctuation entirely (C), mix comma and dash (B), or use a colon inappropriately (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
